diagrams.title: Memory Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchy of storage from fastest/smallest to slowest/largest
diagrams.purpose: Understand that faster memory costs more and is smaller; programs should maximize locality to use faster levels

diagrams.title: Address Translation with MMU
diagrams.description: Shows logical address from CPU being translated to physical address using relocation register
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how MMU provides memory protection and allows process relocation without changing program code

diagrams.title: Contiguous Memory Allocation - Fixed vs Variable
diagrams.description: Compares fixed-size partitions with internal fragmentation vs variable partitions with external fragmentation
elements: Fixed: Memory divided into equal 64KB blocks, some with wasted space inside
Variable: Memory divided based on process needs, gaps between allocated blocks
Free Hole: Unallocated memory space between processes
diagrams.purpose: Understand the difference between internal and external fragmentation and when each occurs

diagrams.title: Swapping Process
diagrams.description: Shows process being moved between main memory and secondary storage
elements: Swap Out: Process moved to disk (swap area)
Swap In: Process moved back to RAM when needed
diagrams.purpose: Understand how swapping enables more processes than physical memory can hold
